name: Dominican Republic (República Dominicana)
summary: The Dominican Republic is a Caribbean civil-law republic whose family-law framework operates under the Civil Code (Código Civil, drawing on French Napoleonic Code substantive heritage via 19th-century adoption) supplemented by the Code for the System of Protection and Fundamental Rights of Children and Adolescents 2003 (Law 136-03, replacing 1994 Code for Minors). Parental authority (autoridad parental) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 371-387 and Law 136-03 Title II. The Supreme Court of Justice (Suprema Corte de Justicia) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional, established 2010) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Children and Adolescents Courts (Tribunales de Niños, Niñas y Adolescentes). Psychology profession is regulated through the Colegio Dominicano de Psicólogos (CODOPSI). The Dominican Republic is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard codified in Law 136-03 art. 5. The Dominican Republic acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 November 2004.

statutory_framework.title: Civil Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Civil Code drawing on French Napoleonic Code substantive heritage via 19th-century adoption. Arts. 371-387 govern autoridad parental and child custody.

statutory_framework.citation: Code for the System of Protection and Fundamental Rights of Children and Adolescents 2003 (Law 136-03)
statutory_framework.title: Code for Children and Adolescents Protection
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Code codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle aligned with UNCRC obligations, replacing 1994 Code for Minors.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Justice (Suprema Corte de Justicia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review (established 2010).

professional_regulators.name: Colegio Dominicano de Psicólogos (CODOPSI)
professional_regulators.role: Federal professional college for psychologists in the Dominican Republic.

anonymisation_convention: Dominican family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Dominican Republic independence + Trinitarios + Haiti-DR separation framework
key_developments.description: Dominican Republic independence 27 February 1844 from Haiti (22-year-Haitian-occupation 1822-1844) — Juan Pablo Duarte and Trinitarios framework. Substantively distinctive globally only-Latin-American-state-with-independence-from-another-Latin-American-state-not-European-power framework. Foundational Hispaniola-shared-island framework affecting subsequent cross-border-jurisdiction-practice.

key_developments.title: Civil Code adoption (French Napoleonic)
key_developments.description: Federal Civil Code adopted via Napoleonic Code substantive heritage. Arts. 371-387 govern autoridad parental and child custody. Foundational substantive-civil-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Trujillo dictatorship + 31-year-authoritarian framework
key_developments.description: Rafael Trujillo dictatorship 1930-1961 — substantively distinctive Caribbean 31-year-authoritarian framework. Substantive 1937 Parsley Massacre (estimated 17,000-35,000 Haitians killed) substantially affecting demographic and family-law-implementation framework. Foundational pre-democratic-transition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: 1965 Dominican Civil War + US intervention + democratic-restoration framework
key_developments.description: 1965 Dominican Civil War 24 April – 3 September 1965 substantively distinctive Caribbean Cold-War-era armed-conflict framework. US-OAS intervention 28 April 1965. Substantive Constitutional restoration framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Dominican Republic ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Dominican Republic ratified the UNCRC on 11 June 1991 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2003 Law 136-03 substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Code for Minors 1994 + pre-2003-framework framework
key_developments.description: Code for Minors enacted 1994 establishing pre-Law-136-03 child-protection framework — substantively significant Caribbean civil-law child-protection-framework. Foundational pre-2003 substantive-framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Law 136-03 — Code for Children and Adolescents Protection
key_developments.description: Federal Code enacted Law 136-03 of 7 August 2003 replacing 1994 Code for Minors, codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle (art. 5) aligned with UNCRC obligations. Substantively significant child-protection-framework consolidation.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession + 2003-banking-crisis context
key_developments.description: The Dominican Republic acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 November 2004 — substantively significant Hague Caribbean accession. Substantive 2003 banking crisis affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-implementation framework.

key_developments.title: Constitution 2010 + Constitutional Court established
key_developments.description: Constitution of the Dominican Republic 2010 adopted 26 January 2010 substantively reforming political-institutional framework. Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional) established with original jurisdiction over constitutional review. Substantive constitutional-judicial framework affecting subsequent family-law-jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Defensor del Pueblo + CONANI + Haiti border crisis framework
key_developments.description: Defensor del Pueblo and CONANI (Consejo Nacional para la Niñez y la Adolescencia) institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Law 136-03 + Civil Code framework. DR-Haiti border crisis 2023-2024 substantively affecting demographic and cross-border family-law-implementation framework. Multiple Suprema Corte decisions developing superior-interest-of-the-child interpretation establishing trajectory toward 2024 Abinader re-election substantive register. Substantively significant Caribbean institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Suprema Corte + Tribunal Constitucional — superior-interest-of-the-child substantive register + Abinader presidency
key_developments.description: Suprema Corte de Justicia and Tribunal Constitucional continue to develop superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code arts. 371-387 + Law 136-03 art. 5 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Luis Abinader (PRM) presidency since 16 August 2020 (re-elected 19 May 2024). Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Dominican Republic operates a French-Napoleonic-derivative civil-law family-law framework — structurally distinctive within the Caribbean cluster (where common-law dominates among Hispaniola, Cuba and Anglophone Caribbean).
Hague Convention 1980 accession 2004 places Dominican Republic in the Hague Caribbean cluster.
Constitutional Court (2010) establishment is among the more recent Latin American constitutional-review-court establishments in the corpus.

sources.title: Judicial Power of the Dominican Republic

editorial_notes: Dominican Republic jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Duarte-to-Abinader trajectory: 1844-Dominican-Republic-independence-+-Trinitarios + 1884-Civil-Code-Napoleonic + 1930-Trujillo-dictatorship-+-31-year-authoritarian + 1965-1965-Dominican-Civil-War-+-US-intervention + 1991-UNCRC-ratification + 1994-Code-for-Minors + 2003-Law-136-03-Children-Adolescents-Protection + 2004-Hague-Convention-1980-accession + 2010-Constitution-+-Constitutional-Court + 2024-Suprema-Corte-+-Abinader-presidency.
Civil-law Caribbean (Civil Code Napoleonic-derivative + Law 136-03 + Constitutional Court 2010 + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2004).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive superior-interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code arts. 371-387 + Law 136-03 art. 5 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Caribbean + civil-law-distinctive-in-Anglophone-Caribbean + Napoleonic-derivative + Trinitarios-Duarte-independence-from-Haiti-only-Latin-American-state-with-independence-from-another-Latin-American-state-globally-distinctive + 22-year-Haitian-occupation-1822-1844 + Hispaniola-shared-island + Trujillo-dictatorship-1930-1961-31-year-authoritarian + 1937-Parsley-Massacre-Haitians + 1965-Dominican-Civil-War-+-US-OAS-intervention + Constitution-2010-+-Constitutional-Court + Hague-Convention-2004 + Abinader-PRM-2020-2024 clusters within the corpus.
